reasoning: Returns 386 hanabi festival entries from Wikidata but most lack prefecture, coordinates, dates, and English descriptions. The count is comprehensive but the data thinness makes it hard for an agent to synthesise a useful answer without significant gaps.
missing_data: Dates/months of each festival, English names, admission info, viewing tips for the agent. Majority of items have null prefecture and null description.
improvement_hint: Enrich hanabi festival records with dates (typically July-August), English name translation, and at least prefecture_code from Wikidata properties before serving.

reasoning: Identical dataset to L3-01 but requested in Japanese. Same coverage of 386 hanabi items; same data thinness problem — null prefecture, null coords, null descriptions for most entries. Lang switch works but underlying record quality limits usefulness.
missing_data: Prefecture codes and date fields for most items; Japanese descriptions for the majority.
improvement_hint: Same as L3-01: backfill hanabi records with dates, prefecture codes and at least Japanese descriptions from Wikidata.

reasoning: User asks for snow festivals in Hokkaido. Tool returns count=0 festival items and falls back to national UNESCO ICH records (Akita Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura etc.) — none of which are snow festivals or Hokkaido. The Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival are absent. Constraint (Hokkaido, snow/winter) is entirely missed.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, yuki-matsuri kind data in the festivals corpus.
improvement_hint: Add yuki/winter festival kind to get_festivals corpus; ensure prefecture filter returns Hokkaido events rather than silently falling back to national heritage.

reasoning: User asks about rural village life experiences in Chinese. Tool retrieves get_japan_heritage without a filter, returning heritage sites (Jo-an tea house, Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama) with no Chinese text. Nothing addresses inaka/rural-stay experiences. zh lang parameter present but most items have no zh fields.
missing_data: Rural accommodation/experience spots (satoyama, farmstay, inaka), zh-language descriptions for returned items.
improvement_hint: For rural-life queries route to search_area with 農村体験 or 古民家 rather than get_japan_heritage; ensure zh translation fields are populated.

reasoning: Korean query for autumn foliage; search returns 140 matches including sakura_meisho_100 sites (Sakurayama Gunma, Kenroku-en, Himeji Castle). Coverage is good though the top results skew toward spots named 紅葉 (the character) rather than famous koyo-viewing spots. No ko-language descriptions present but structure is sound.
missing_data: Korean-language name/description fields; top famous koyo spots like Nikko, Arashiyama ranked higher.
improvement_hint: Add ko translations to top attraction records; tune semantic match to surface foliage-viewing spots by season tag rather than only character-match.

reasoning: User wants a quiet, non-touristy meditation spot. Response returns 修行-named temples and a DMO event (Rinzai-ji zazen experience in Shizuoka) and a ninja experience. Some relevant items exist but no filtering for 'quiet/non-touristy' — Sugyoji Tokyo is a city temple. Constraint of non-touristy is not served.
missing_data: Quiet mountain temples offering shukubo (temple lodging) or zazen; crowd_band=low filter applied to temple results.
improvement_hint: Use indoor_capable and crowd_band fields to surface quiet/low-crowd temple options when query implies non-touristy preference.

reasoning: User asks (in Spanish) to see auroras in Japan. Aurora borealis is essentially impossible to guarantee in Japan (very rare in Hokkaido). Response returns observatories and national parks via kinds_class_match (dark_sky) but does not flag the near-impossibility of auroras in Japan. Results include Okinawa/subtropical parks with no aurora relevance.
missing_data: Factual caveat that auroras are extremely rare in Japan; if relevant, Hokkaido dark-sky spots. Okinawa results are irrelevant noise.
wrong_data: Yanbaru (Okinawa) and Iriomote as aurora locations is factually misleading.
improvement_hint: Add safety/feasibility note for aurora queries; filter national-park results by latitude (Hokkaido preferred); suppress subtropical results for high-latitude phenomena.

reasoning: User wants to sleep in a 100-year-old house (古民家). Tool returns Gifu hotels — mostly onsen ryokan (hito_yu_kai) and OSM-sourced hostels. No machiya/kominka lodging type identified. 古民家 constraint is unaddressed; data layer doesn't appear to have kominka-specific lodging type.
missing_data: kominka/machiya lodging type; Shirakawa-go gassho-zukuri farmhouse stays in Gifu which are iconic 100-year-old houses.
improvement_hint: Add kominka/machiya as a lodging_type tag; ingest Shirakawa-go farmhouse accommodation listings from official tourism sources.

reasoning: Thai query for non-touristy cherry blossom spots. Returns 215 sakura matches including less-known Sakurayama (Gunma) and Matsumae Castle (Hokkaido) which are valid. However top results are still Kenroku-en, Himeji Castle — famous/crowded. 'Not crowded' constraint not applied; crowd_band field exists but not used to filter.
missing_data: crowd_band=low filtering; Thai-language name/descriptions for top results.
improvement_hint: When query intent implies off-the-beaten-path, apply crowd_band=low preference in ranking; add th-language description translation pass.

reasoning: Photography / dramatic landscapes query; returns 53 絶景 results including DMO curated lists (Ehime, Niigata) with named specific spots. DMO content provides good context even if individual spot-level detail varies. Adequate for an agent to build a useful answer.
missing_data: Specific named spots with coordinates rather than DMO list-page links; English translations of most results.
improvement_hint: Extract individual named spots from DMO 絶景 lists into discrete records with coordinates to improve specificity.

reasoning: Arabic query for outdoor onsen in nature (not hotels). Returns 72 露天風呂 results — several are outdoor hot springs (Hosen-bo, Haraigawa Onsen) with nature context. However the constraint 'not in hotels' is not filtered; most results are traditional onsen facilities attached to ryokan or hotels. Arabic lang param present but no ar translations.
missing_data: ar-language descriptions; filtering by outdoor/natural露天 vs hotel-attached baths; wild/soto-yu listings.
improvement_hint: Tag onsen spots as 'wild/natural' vs 'hotel-attached' to enable constraint filtering for open-air nature bathing queries.

reasoning: User wants to swim with wild dolphins. Top result is a Kyoto tourism ambassador page (completely irrelevant). Second result is a Noto aquarium (captive dolphins). Third is a fish market. No wild dolphin swim spots (Mikurajima, Amami) appear. Core retrieval failure for a niche but real Japan tourism activity.
missing_data: Mikurajima (Tokyo) wild dolphin swimming, Amami Oshima, Tsuji Marine in Kochi — well-known wild dolphin spots in Japan.
improvement_hint: Add イルカウォッチング / 野生イルカ / dolphin_watching kind tag; ingest DMO pages from Mikurajima and Amami covering this experience.

reasoning: Query targets traditional architecture untouched by modernization (重伝建 / preservation districts). Tool calls get_japan_heritage returning 104 heritage stories, but top_canonical_heritage shows Jo-an tea house, Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama — none are townscape preservation districts. The right data exists (preservation_district kind in search_area) but wrong tool was chosen.
missing_data: Preservation district results (妻籠宿, 奈良井宿, Gion, Uchiko); tool should have been search_area with preservation_district kind or search term 重伝建.
improvement_hint: Route 'traditional townscape' queries to search_area with preservation_district kind filter rather than get_japan_heritage.

reasoning: Vietnamese query for Ainu culture in Hokkaido. Returns 129 results with Ainu Museum (former Porotokotan), National Ainu Museum (Upopoy), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Hokkaido Ainu Center, and Kawamura Kaneto Museum — all highly relevant, Hokkaido-located, with coordinates and websites. Excellent coverage and specificity for this niche topic.
missing_data: vi-language descriptions for the returned items; craft/performance experience details (Ainu embroidery workshops etc.).
improvement_hint: Add vi-language translation pass for Hokkaido Ainu sites; include Ainu cultural experience (crafts, dance) spots from DMO sources.

reasoning: Query for cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏). Top results are Usuki Stone Buddhas (Oita, National Treasure), Oya-dera (Tochigi), Komasaka Magaibutsu (Shiga) — all highly relevant, named, with coordinates and heritage designations. Good geographic spread. One item lacks English name but is still searchable.
missing_data: English descriptions for lesser-known sites; visiting hours/access info for Usuki and Oya.
improvement_hint: Add opening_hours and access notes for top-tier magaibutsu sites; ensure English name translation for unnamed items.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for day-trip volcano hikes from Tokyo. Results include Iwate (Tohoku, ~5h), Unzen (Nagasaki, >10h), Lake Towada — all infeasible day trips from Tokyo. Mt Fuji appears but buried (rank 2). Mihara (Oshima), Omuroyama (Izu), Hakone — ideal Tokyo-accessible volcanoes — not prominently surfaced. Distance/feasibility constraint ignored.
missing_data: Oshima/Mihara, Omuroyama (Izu), Hakone volcanic zone, Nikko Shirane — all day-trip feasible from Tokyo.
improvement_hint: Add geographic proximity filter or Tokyo-distance scoring when day-trip constraint is detected; promote Kanto/Tokai volcanoes.

reasoning: Indonesian query for making fresh tofu at a temple. Returns MAFF GI frozen tofu product, Iki island tofu culture, and a mountain tofu shop at Kongo-san — interesting but no temple tofu experience. Koya-san (famous for koya-dofu and shojin ryori tofu) is absent. 'At a temple' constraint unaddressed.
missing_data: Koya-san temple tofu/shojin ryori experience; Zen temple tofu making workshops (e.g. Eiheiji, Ryoanji). id-language descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Add shojin_ryori and temple_food experience tags to relevant temple spots; index Koya-san tofu and Eiheiji cuisine experiences.

reasoning: 'Fishing villages frozen in time' query. Returns Itoman Port (Okinawa — active fishing), Teradomari fish market (Niigata), Shiraishi fishing port closure notice. Some relevant content (Itoman with traditional haree racing) but description-heavy content is noisy. Missing classic preservation fishing villages like Ine no Funaya (Kyoto) or Sawara.
missing_data: Ine no Funaya (Kyoto), Dogashima (Shizuoka), classic preservation-listed fishing hamlets. fishing_village kind results not well-represented.
improvement_hint: Boost preservation_district + fishing_village kind combination in search ranking; add Ine-no-Funaya explicitly via Wikidata.

reasoning: Query: learn washi paper making from a master. Tool returns METI craft designation list — 231 craft items starting with Ainu textiles, Oitama silk, Ugo shina cloth. Washi-specific items (Echizen washi, Mino washi) may exist deeper in the list but are not surfaced in the truncated 4000-char preview. No experience/workshop info at all. 'From a master' workshop constraint not addressed.
missing_data: Washi paper designations (Echizen, Mino, Ogawa washi); workshop/experiential info linking to producers. q= filter for 和紙 not applied.
improvement_hint: Apply q='和紙' filter to get_local_specialty for paper-making queries; add experience_available flag to craft records where hands-on workshops are offered.

reasoning: Japanese query for period-drama-like scenery (時代劇). Tool uses 宿場 keyword and returns excellent results: Tsumago-juku (#1, must_see), Narai-juku, Seki-juku — all Important Preservation Districts. DMO scrape adds Kiso road narrative content. Highly relevant, well-structured, with coordinates and heritage designations.
missing_data: A few more post-town names (Magome, Hakone-juku) would enrich coverage; English translations for non-Japanese speakers.
improvement_hint: Add Magome-juku and Hakone-juku to shukuba kind coverage; cross-link DMO scenic route content with individual spot records.

reasoning: 'Not crowded' beach query. Returns 115 results including Naminoue (Okinawa — urban/crowded), Tropical Beach (Okinawa), and many other Okinawa beaches. All have crowd_band=medium or low but the 'not crowded' filter isn't applied in ranking. Less-known beaches on Sea of Japan coast or remote islands would better serve intent.
missing_data: Remote island beaches (Iriomote, Yoron, Tokunoshima) prioritised; crowd_band=low filtering applied to surface genuinely quieter options.
improvement_hint: Rank by crowd_band=low when 'not crowded' detected; elevate remote-island beach results over urban Okinawa beaches.

reasoning: 'Small old-school izakaya with locals' query. Returns Yokocho Park (a park in Shinagawa), a Saga tourism page in Chinese, and theme-park-style 横丁 attractions. Actual drinking alleys (Omoide Yokocho, Ebisu Yokocho, Golden Gai, Yurakucho) are absent. Real izakaya street data is largely missing; data ranking noise buries any relevant results.
missing_data: Omoide Yokocho, Shinjuku Golden Gai, Ebisu Yokocho, Yurakucho izakaya alley — most famous examples.
improvement_hint: Add yokocho kind with specific famous alley records (Omoide Yokocho, Golden Gai) from Wikipedia/Wikidata; ensure these rank above parks incidentally tagged yokocho.

reasoning: German query for scenic local train travel in rural Japan. Returns 74 results including Sagano Scenic Line (Kyoto), Oigawa Main Line (Shizuoka), local railways in Tohoku, and a rich Nagano DMO feature on the Bessho Line. Good coverage of local_railway kind. De-language descriptions absent but intent is well served.
missing_data: de-language descriptions; famous scenic railways like Iiyama Line, Gono Line, Kiha diesel trains could be highlighted.
improvement_hint: Add de translations for top scenic railway records; include additional JR scenic local lines (Gono, Iiyama, Shimanto) with route description.

reasoning: Firefly viewing in summer. Top result is Hotaruka (squid — ホタルイカ — not firefly), which is a natural monument in Toyama but mismatched by kanji overlap. Second result is Hotaru-no-Sato Park, Hyogo; third is a protected firefly habitat in Tokushima. Also returns Toyoda Hotaru Museum and a DMO blurb about Hotaru-bune in Shimonoseki — genuinely useful.
missing_data: Dedicated firefly viewing spot records from Shikoku/Kansai/Kanto with viewing season (June-July). Hotaruka squid should be ranked lower for firefly queries.
improvement_hint: Disambiguate ホタルイカ (firefly squid) vs ホタル (firefly) by kind tag; add viewing season metadata to hotaru spots.

reasoning: Russian query for wintering cranes in Japan. Top result Izumi crane migration grounds (Kagoshima) is the #1 answer with Special Natural Monument designation, correct coordinates, Ramsar wetland — excellent. Kushiro-shitsugen (Hokkaido, red-crowned crane habitat) also appears. Many municipality name matches (鶴田, 鶴居村) add noise but key sites are present.
missing_data: Tsurui village (鶴居村) linked to red-crowned crane observation; viewing platform details; ru-language descriptions.
wrong_data: Yanbaru/subtropical parks via kinds_class_match national_park are irrelevant noise.
improvement_hint: Prioritise crane_wintering kind over generic national_park kind for crane queries; suppress geographic noise from municipality name matches.
